task_completion.visual_evidence: The FreeMED dashboard or main interface is visible, indicating a successful login.

task_completion.description: Locate and open the chart for patient Thomas Anderson
task_completion.visual_evidence: The patient dashboard, summary, or chart specifically for 'Thomas Anderson' is open and visible.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the surgical history or procedures section and open the add form
task_completion.visual_evidence: A form, module, or dialog box for adding a new surgical history or procedure is open on the screen.

task_completion.description: Enter the correct procedure name and date
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Laparoscopic Cholecystectomy' is entered into the procedure/surgery name field, and '2018-06-15' is entered into the date field.

task_completion.description: Enter the correct facility and notes
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Mercy General Hospital' is entered into the surgeon/facility/location field, and 'Routine gallbladder removal. No complications.' is entered into the notes/comments field.
A confirmation/success message is displayed, or the newly added surgical record is visible in the patient's surgical history list or table.

integrity.description: Agent used the FreeMED web interface
integrity.visual_evidence: All interactions occur within a web browser displaying the FreeMED application, not via terminal commands, database scripts, or API calls.

integrity.description: Agent entered data into the actual application
integrity.visual_evidence: The surgical details are typed into the actual FreeMED form fields, not into a text editor, notepad, or search bar.
The record appears in the system's history table or triggers a standard application success message, without evidence of DOM manipulation via browser developer tools.
